dataset: CAMEO Chemicals / Methyl Acrylate Synthesis Process

pi_items.verified_value: Pilot-Scale Methyl Acrylate Synthesis (Fischer Esterification Route)
pi_items.source: synthesis_process_description.txt confirms process name and esterification route

pi_items.verified_value: All 5 chemicals verified with CAS numbers: Acrylic Acid (79-10-7), Sulfuric Acid (7664-93-9), Methanol (67-56-1), Sodium Hydroxide (1310-73-2), Toluene (108-88-3)
pi_items.source: synthesis_process_description.txt lists all chemicals with CAS and UN numbers in process steps

pi_items.verified_value: Acrylic Acid + Sodium Hydroxide (Step 5 of process: neutralization step)
pi_items.source: synthesis_process_description.txt Step 5; CAMEO Chemicals confirms strong bases cause violent polymerization with acrylic acid

pi_items.verified_value: Violent exothermic neutralization; can trigger uninhibited polymerization of acrylic acid
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals database confirms acrylic acid undergoes explosive polymerization with strong bases. Multiple sources (CDC NIOSH, research papers) confirm violent exothermic nature and polymerization risk when acrylic acid contacts strong bases like NaOH

pi_items.verified_value: Methanol + Sulfuric Acid (Steps 2-3 of process: catalyst addition then methanol metering)
pi_items.source: synthesis_process_description.txt process steps and assignment section explicitly identifies this as a pair to assess

pi_items.metadata_value: Exothermic, generates dimethyl ether at elevated temp
pi_items.verified_value: Exothermic reaction; at elevated temperatures can produce dimethyl ether (flammable gas)
pi_items.source: Web search results confirm methanol + concentrated sulfuric acid at elevated temperatures (250-350°C range) can produce dimethyl ether. Research on methanol dehydration catalysis confirms this reaction pathway. Dimethyl ether is classified as flammable gas.

pi_items.metadata_value: Building 12 lacks explosion-proof ventilation for flammable vapors
pi_items.verified_value: Building 12 has 'No dedicated explosion-proof ventilation; general building HVAC only' and contains ignition sources (ovens, hot plates)
pi_items.source: synthesis_process_description.txt BUILDING 12 CONTEXT section explicitly confirms lack of explosion-proof ventilation and presence of ignition sources in same building

pi_items.verified_value: Discrepancy found: synthesis_process_description.txt instructs saving to ~/Documents/process_hazard_analysis.txt (line 60), but task.json and verifier expect /home/ga/Documents/process_hazard_report.txt
pi_items.source: synthesis_process_description.txt line 60 says 'process_hazard_analysis.txt' while task.json metadata and verifier.py look for 'process_hazard_report.txt'
setup_task.sh line 30 copies the file to this location; task description references ~/Desktop/synthesis_process_description.txt

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic occupational health and safety (OHS) training task for methyl acrylate synthesis using CAMEO Chemicals. The process involves 5 chemicals: Acrylic Acid, Sulfuric Acid (catalyst), Methanol (reactant), Sodium Hydroxide (neutralizer), and Toluene (solvent). Two critical hazardous chemical pairs have been verified: (1) Acrylic Acid + Sodium Hydroxide produces violent exothermic reactions and can trigger uninhibited polymerization of acrylic acid, confirmed by CAMEO Chemicals and research literature; (2) Methanol + Sulfuric Acid at elevated temperatures generates dimethyl ether (flammable gas), confirmed by industrial chemistry research. The process description identifies a safety gap: Building 12 lacks explosion-proof ventilation despite containing flammable chemicals and ignition sources. A critical file path discrepancy exists: the process data file instructs saving the report as 'process_hazard_analysis.txt' but the task expects 'process_hazard_report.txt', which could cause task failure if agents follow the data file instructions.
